steps.title: Identify the radii
steps.content: Since $Q$ and $R$ lie on the circle with center $P$ and radius 9 inches, both $PQ$ and $PR$ are radii of the circle. Therefore, $PQ = 9$ and $PR = 9$.

steps.title: Apply the perimeter constraint
steps.content:
The perimeter of triangle $PQR$ is the sum of all three sides:
$$PQ + PR + QR = 31$$
Substituting the known values:
$$9 + 9 + QR = 31$$
$$18 + QR = 31$$
$$QR = 13$$
That confirms B is correct.
